framework.name: MCP vs A2A Architecture Pattern
framework.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) handles LLM-to-tool communication within a single agent system. A2A handles agent-to-agent communication for inter-agent collaboration. The car repair analogy: MCP is the mechanic using their tools; A2A is the mechanic talking to the customer and parts supplier. This creates a layered architecture where agents internally use MCP for tools and externally use A2A for collaboration.
framework.raw_quote: MCP is a protocol that helps LLM communicate with tools, resources, prompt, and so on. But in real life, agent will communicate with another agent
